sources.title: Have you ever accepted a promotion where you had to move to another state and leave family and friends behind? - Quora
sources.content: Answer (1 of 69): Yes. I thought it would be wonderful. My daughter came with me and my son was in college. I had been divorced for several years. When I got there, I found out the job was not as originally presented. My daughter hated it and wanted to leave to go live with her father . The peopl...

sources.title: I Was Offered a Life-Changing Promotion, but My Partner Refuses to Relocate / Bright Side
sources.content: October 12, 2025 - If he cannot respect your ambition now, when your dreams are finally within reach, what happens later when you face other major life decisions like buying a home, starting a family, or handling financial risks? Choosing your promotion doesn’t mean you’re throwing away love, it means you’re refusing to sacrifice your future to someone unwilling to grow alongside you.

sources.title: Should you move for a job? — Remote-First Coaching
sources.content: June 28, 2025 - I’m fortunate to continue to ... Sweeney, CEO, MyCorporation.com · "Rather than moving away from family for a job, I moved away from my job in order to be closer to family....

sources.title: 13 Signs It's Time For You To Move Away To A New City, Because There Are So Many Reasons For A Fresh Start
sources.content: March 11, 2015 - You don't necessarily have to move to reach peak success in your career, but it certainly helps, and it'll be a relief to live near people who actually know what you're talking about for once. It doesn't matter how old you are or how long it's been—if you feel like you want to move and be closer to the humans you love, there's no shame in that. Not everybody gets merrily along with the 'rents and sibs. If you've got the guts to strike it out on your own, more power to you. Even if you do get along with your family, sometimes it's nice to get a break and get a sense for who you are on your own.

sources.title: should I give up on job-searching in my new city and move back home? - Ask a Manager
sources.content: March 3, 2021 - I didn’t judge her when she told me they didn’t want to have a baby 1,000s of miles away from their extended families and with no support network in place – it made sense to me. ... I do think part of the reason I’m afraid to move back is the judgement I’m scared of facing. I made a big deal about moving across the country and if I go back, I’m worried about what people will think (which I know is the silliest thing ever since it’s my life and career, and not theirs), but also, I guess it’s to be expected when the world has changed so much in the past few months.
